The Thesis

The window is open. It won't stay open.

AI compute demand is doubling every 6 months. Grid interconnection queues in Virginia and Texas are 4+ years. Gas interconnection requests for AI factories jumped 160% year-over-year. The industry is moving to on-site natural gas generation — and nobody is closer to the source than us.

POWER ADVANTAGE

3-5 cents/kWh

Behind-the-meter nat gas generation at Henry Hub pricing. Virginia pays 10-12 cents. Texas pays 8 cents with spike risk. California pays 27 cents. This gap widens every year.

MARKET GAP

First Mover

Zero regional AI compute facilities between Houston and Jacksonville. The entire Gulf Coast — oil & gas, petrochemical, defense, agriculture, ports — is sending data to Virginia. That's ending.

INCENTIVES

Louisiana Wants This

LED Act 730 digital infrastructure credits. Parish-level PILOT tax agreements. IRA energy credits. SBA financing programs. The state is actively funding this buildout.

⛽
CHEAPEST POWER
Nat gas at source pricing. 40 miles from Henry Hub. No utility middleman. 50-65% below national average.
🚫
ZERO COMPETITION
No AI compute infrastructure exists in the Lafayette-to-Lake Charles corridor. The entire Gulf Coast is underserved. We're first.
🎯
MISSION CRITICAL
O&G, defense, autonomous systems, first responders — Gulf Coast industries need local AI compute NOW. Cloud latency doesn't cut it.
💰
STACKED INCENTIVES
LED Act 730. PILOT tax agreements. IRA energy credits. SBA programs. Louisiana wants this built — and they're paying to make it happen.
Energy Efficiency

Legacy facilities waste 40% of their power on cooling. We waste less than 3%.

Every ADC facility is built to NVIDIA's DSX reference design with integrated liquid cooling and renewable-first power architecture. NVIDIA ships the racks. We build the facility. Every watt goes to compute.

COOLING

PUE 1.03

NVIDIA integrated liquid cooling. Industry average PUE is 1.58. Our facilities target 1.05-1.15 — liquid cooling handles GPU heat, minimal auxiliary fans, no massive HVAC. 95% of every dollar of power goes directly to AI compute.

POWER

3–5¢

On-site natural gas generation at Henry Hub pricing — the lowest energy cost of any AI facility in the United States. Solar offset on every site. Battery backup standard. Grid is backup, never primary.

RENEWABLE

Solar First

Every site deploys solar arrays and battery storage as the primary daytime power source. Natural gas handles baseload. Willow Glen's existing tank farm enables future green hydrogen — 100% renewable path.

Why this matters: A 2 MW AI facility with PUE 1.58 (industry average) wastes 580 kW just on cooling. That's $400K/year burned on moving air. At PUE 1.03, we waste 30 kW. The savings compound every hour of every day — and they go straight to the bottom line.

NVIDIA Platform

Seven Chips. One AI Supercomputer.

The Vera Rubin platform is not a single GPU — it is seven purpose-built chips working as one system. Every ADC3K facility is designed around this architecture.

Seven Chips — One AI Supercomputer Official NVIDIA Product Renders — vera-rubin-nvl72
RUBIN GPU

288 GB HBM4 — 22 TB/s memory bandwidth. The inference engine at the core of every NVL72 rack. 72 per rack.

VERA CPU

88 custom Olympus cores. Replaces x86 entirely for AI workload orchestration. 36 per rack, paired with Rubin GPUs.

NVLINK 6 SWITCH

3.6 TB/s per-GPU bidirectional NVLink bandwidth. 260 TB/s aggregate within a single NVL72 rack. PCIe is not in this equation.

CONNECTX-9 SUPERNIC

1.6 Tb/s aggregate per GPU. AI traffic between nodes, racks, and storage — zero CPU involvement.

BLUEFIELD-4 DPU

Security and infrastructure at line rate. Encryption, firewall, storage I/O offloaded — GPU cycles go to inference only.

SPECTRUM-X ETHERNET

800G per port — co-packaged optics. Scale-out factory fabric built for AI east-west traffic at rack scale.

LPX
GROQ 3
GROQ 3 LPX

Language Processing Unit — 500 MB on-chip SRAM, 640 TB/s bandwidth. The decode engine: GPU handles prefill, Groq handles fast token output. 35x tokens-per-watt. Dynamo 1.0 orchestrates both. This is the multiplier.

GPU-swap architecture: pod infrastructure remains. Compute upgrades without replacing the facility.

The ADC3K Ecosystem

Three platforms, fully integrated.

MARLIE I is the brain. ADC 3K pods are the body. KLFT extends into autonomous airspace. Together — Louisiana's first vertically integrated AI infrastructure company.

The Problem
Your AI workload is 1,000 miles
from your operation.

Virginia. Oregon. Texas mega-campuses. Your data travels across the country, waits in queue, and comes back on someone else's timeline. Autonomous systems can't wait 50ms. Industrial process control can't tolerate that variability. The Gulf Coast has zero regional AI compute infrastructure.

The Fix
Put the compute where
the work is.

ADC builds physical AI infrastructure and deploys it regionally. Not a cloud company. Not a reseller. We manufacture the hardware, pour the pads, pull the wire, and turn it on. Your data never leaves the Gulf Coast.

Two Deployment Architectures

Racks today. Pods tomorrow. Both on NVIDIA.

We don't ask you to wait. ADC offers two paths to deployment — both on the same NVIDIA Vera Rubin stack, same InfiniBand spine, same Louisiana tax incentives.

OPTION A — ENTERPRISE RACKS
Building-Ready. Operational in Weeks.

NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 racks deployed in existing industrial space. NVIDIA's integrated rack-scale liquid cooling — minimal facility buildout. We are a licensed Louisiana general contractor. We pull the permits, do the buildout, take delivery. Willow Glen has a 20,000 SF warehouse ready now.

OPTION B — ADC 3K PODS
Containerized. Deploy Anywhere.

DSX-compliant AI facility modules inside ISO shipping containers. NVIDIA liquid-cooled racks, self-powered, remotely managed. Drop on a concrete pad anywhere — wetlands, offshore, industrial sites, airports. Deployable neocloud nodes. The differentiated product.

Same spine. Same power. Same tax stack.   Both options connect to NVIDIA Quantum InfiniBand in fat-tree topology. Option A funds Option B. The fabric grows either way.
NOW ACCEPTING DEPLOYMENT INQUIRIES
The Product

ADC 3K — Modular AI Compute Pod

The ADC 3K pod is a DSX-compliant AI facility module inside a 40-foot ISO shipping container. NVIDIA liquid-cooled racks, power distribution, facility water loop, networking, and physical security — fully self-contained. A deployable neocloud node.

Drop it on a concrete pad, connect power and fiber, and it's operational. No building permits. No construction. Order to operational: approximately 16 weeks.

3.0 MW
Class IT Load
1.05
PUE
0
Gallons Water/Year
16 wk
Order to Operational

5-Zone Interior Layout

ZONE 1

Compute

NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 racks with integrated liquid cooling (45°C hot water, 2-hour install). Dynamo 1.0 inference OS + Groq LPX decode racks. Full BMS telemetry + TraceTek leak detection.

ZONE 2

Power Distribution

Medium-voltage input, transformer, PDUs. ATS switchgear for automatic failover between power layers. Vertiv GXT5 LFP UPS on-board for instant ride-through.

ZONE 3

Networking

NVIDIA Quantum InfiniBand NDR fabric. NVLink + NVSwitch inside the pod, ConnectX-7 NICs connect up to the spine. All telemetry streams to MARLIE I NOC via DCGM + UFM in real time.

ZONE 4

Heat Rejection

External dry cooler array rejects heat from facility water loop. Cooling towers where river water is available. Dehumidification + ambient airflow for rack-level fans and non-liquid-cooled components. Quiet operation.

ZONE 5

Safety & Security

ISO container structure. Biometric access, cameras, tamper sensors. VESDA aspirating detection + FK-5-1-12 clean agent fire suppression. Data never leaves unless you send it.

ARCHITECTURE

DGX SuperPOD Fabric

Each pod is a leaf on an NVIDIA Quantum InfiniBand spine. Fat-tree topology — add pods to scale wide, inject pods to eliminate bottlenecks. AI-managed fabric grows organically. No upper limit.

Power Stack — Every Pod

LAYER 1 — NAT GAS + SOLAR + BATTERY
Prime-rated natural gas gensets running 24/7 on Henry Hub pricing. Solar offsets daytime fuel burn. Battery provides instant ATS bridge during switching — zero perceived downtime.
PRIMARY
LAYER 2 — GRID
Local utility grid connection for maintenance windows and backup. Never primary — keeps cost structure independent of rate increases.
BACKUP
LAYER 3 — DIESEL
On-site diesel genset. Pipeline-independent. Hurricane-rated. Activates when gas and grid are both compromised.
EMERGENCY
How It Scales

Modular DGX SuperPOD. One NOC. Unlimited pods.

Every ADC 3K pod is a SuperPOD building block on an NVIDIA Quantum InfiniBand spine. Fat-tree topology — add pods to scale compute wide, inject pods to eliminate bottlenecks. NVIDIA DCGM + UFM monitor the entire fabric in real time from MARLIE I. The fabric grows organically. Each new pod is pure margin growth.

MARLIE I — THE NOC
Headquarters + Command Center

1201 SE Evangeline Thruway, Lafayette. Permanent AI factory with NVIDIA NVL72 racks and CDU liquid cooling. Runs NVIDIA Base Command Manager, DCGM, and UFM — the brain that orchestrates the entire SuperPOD fabric across all deployed pods 24/7.

DEPLOYED PODS
Drop Anywhere. Managed Centrally.

Industrial sites, pipeline corridors, airports, university campuses, military installations — wherever compute is needed. 16 weeks from order to operational. No building permits. No construction crew on-site.

The math is simple.   Fixed NOC cost + each new pod's recurring compute revenue = margins that widen with every deployment. No new buildings. No new staff. Just more pods on more pads.
Engineering

The technology stack
behind ADC3K.

NVIDIA integrated liquid cooling, 3-layer resilient power, and multi-path connectivity. Every system engineered for continuous industrial operation.

01 — COOLING

Why liquid cooling changes everything.

Modern AI chips generate extreme thermal density. A single AI GPU produces heat equivalent to a kitchen toaster, concentrated into a chip the size of your hand. Air cooling physically cannot remove heat fast enough at these power densities.

NVIDIA's integrated liquid cooling delivers 45°C hot water directly to every rack — removing heat 1,200x more efficiently than air alone. Liquid handles 80%+ of thermal load. Internal rack fans manage remaining airflow over power supplies and networking. No massive HVAC. No chillers. 2-hour install per rack.

Traditional Air Cooling

The Old Way

Computer room air conditioners. PUE 1.4-1.6 — 40-60% of power wasted on cooling. Raised floors, hot/cold aisle containment, massive HVAC. Fails above ~1 kW per rack density.

ADC 3K — NVIDIA Liquid Cooling

The New Way

NVIDIA integrated liquid cooling at every rack. 45°C hot water, 2-hour install. PUE 1.05-1.15 — minimal overhead. Liquid handles GPU heat, internal fans for auxiliary components. No massive HVAC systems. 100 kW+ per rack. Factory-standard.

1.05
PUE vs 1.5 Industry Avg
0
Gallons Water Per Year
1,200x
Better Heat Transfer vs Air
30%
Lower Cooling OpEx
NVIDIA INTEGRATED LIQUID COOLING

Factory-standard. Rack-level. 2-hour install.

Rack-Integrated

Cooling built into every NVIDIA rack. 45°C hot water return. No custom engineering — factory standard.

2-Hour Install

Each rack connects to the facility water loop in under 2 hours. Plug and cool. Scale by adding racks.

100+ kW Per Rack

Handles the full thermal load of Vera Rubin NVL72 (72 GPUs). No thermal throttling. No density limits.

DSX Compliant

Built to NVIDIA's DSX reference design. Validated architecture. Omniverse digital twin for facility planning.

02 — POWER

3-Layer Power Architecture — every ADC3K site.

Every site runs the same three-layer stack. Natural gas + solar + battery is Layer 1 — primary, continuous, 24/7. The grid is Layer 2 — backup for maintenance and low-power ops. Diesel is Layer 3 — hurricane-rated emergency power. Built around the lessons of Hurricane Ida.

LAYER 1 — NAT GAS + SOLAR + BATTERY
PRIMARY · Prime-rated · 24/7 · Henry Hub pricing. Natural gas gensets run continuously as primary power. Solar offsets daytime fuel burn. Battery provides instant ATS bridge during switching (~30-50ms).
PRIMARY
LAYER 2 — UTILITY GRID
BACKUP · Low-power ops · Maintenance windows. LUS grid connection handles office, NOC, and monitoring loads. Steps in as backup if primary system needs servicing.
BACKUP
LAYER 3 — DIESEL GENSETS
EMERGENCY · On-site fuel · Hurricane-rated. Diesel with on-site fuel storage. Activates when both gas pipelines and grid are compromised — as happened during Hurricane Ida.
EMERGENCY

MARLIE I: 2× 1,500 kW nat gas (N+1) + 1× 1,500 kW diesel + 300 kW solar + 600 kWh battery.   Pod sites: 2× 750 kW nat gas (N+1) + 1× 750 kW diesel + 200 kW solar + 400 kWh battery.

03 — CONNECTIVITY

Three uplink paths — including space.

PATH 1

Fiber Optic

Primary path. Direct fiber backhaul to MARLIE I and internet exchange. Multi-gigabit. Lowest latency. Required at all deployment sites where available.

PATH 2

Licensed Microwave

Secondary path. Point-to-point microwave radio links. Activated automatically if fiber fails. Full management plane within milliseconds.

PATH 3

Starlink

Tertiary and last-resort uplink. Satellite means even remote or disaster-affected sites maintain NOC visibility — critical for hurricane recovery scenarios.

Security & First Response

Protecting infrastructure.
Protecting communities.

Every ADC3K site ships with a layered security system — AI-powered software, autonomous drone patrols, and a first responder network that turns our infrastructure into a community asset.

01 — FIRST RESPONDERS

AI infrastructure that serves the community first.

This is the part nobody else in AI infrastructure is talking about. Every ADC3K site is a first responder force multiplier. When hurricanes hit, when floods rise, when emergencies strike — our drones, our power, our connectivity, and our compute are already on the ground, already operational.

HURRICANE RESPONSE

Aerial Damage Assessment

Skydio drones deploy within minutes of storm passage. Autonomous flight patterns map damage across parishes — downed power lines, flooded roads, structural collapse. Data to emergency managers in hours, not days.

SEARCH & RESCUE

Thermal & Visual Search

Infrared + visual cameras locate stranded residents on rooftops, in floodwaters, in collapsed structures. Autonomous waypoint navigation covers search grids systematically — no pilot fatigue, no missed sectors.

COMMUNITY POWER

Emergency Power & Connectivity

Our 3-layer power stack keeps running when the grid goes down. Nat gas + diesel + solar = self-sufficient. Starlink uplink maintains communications. We become the emergency operations hub for the community.

FIRE DEPARTMENTS

Pre-Incident Planning

Drone-generated 3D site maps shared with local fire departments before any emergency. Thermal monitoring detects hotspots on infrastructure — tank farms, substations, solar arrays — before they become fires.

LAW ENFORCEMENT

Perimeter & Pursuit Support

Autonomous perimeter patrols provide real-time video feeds to parish law enforcement. Industrial corridor surveillance that would otherwise require multiple officers — handled by one drone dock.

The pitch to every city and parish: ADC3K doesn't just create jobs and tax revenue. We deploy a first responder drone network, emergency power infrastructure, and satellite communications — at zero cost to the community. That's the deal no other AI infrastructure company is offering.

02 — NVIDIA AI SECURITY PLATFORM

Software-defined security. Hardware-accelerated.

Every ADC3K pod runs the NVIDIA AI Enterprise security stack — centralized license management, node-locked authentication, role-based access control, and continuous monitoring. The same platform protecting the world's most sensitive AI workloads.

LICENSING & AUTH

NVIDIA NLS

Centralized license management via NVIDIA Licensing Portal. Cloud + local license pools. Floating and node-locked licenses. User roles and authentication at every layer. No unauthorized GPU access — ever.

MONITORING

Continuous Oversight

Real-time GPU utilization, thermal, and workload monitoring across every pod in the network. Anomaly detection flags unauthorized compute patterns. MARLIE I NOC has eyes on every rack, every pod, every site — 24/7.

LAYER 1 — IDENTITY
NVIDIA NLS user roles, node-locked licenses, multi-factor authentication. No anonymous compute.
ACTIVE
LAYER 2 — NETWORK
Encrypted pod-to-NOC tunnels. Segmented VLANs per tenant. Zero-trust architecture. Starlink failover for management plane.
ACTIVE
LAYER 3 — PHYSICAL
Skydio X10 autonomous patrols. Thermal + visual cameras. Perimeter intrusion detection. 24/7 autonomous coverage.
ACTIVE
LAYER 4 — RESPONSE
Automated alerts to MARLIE I NOC + local law enforcement. Video evidence chain. First responder data sharing.
ACTIVE
03 — SKYDIO AUTONOMOUS DRONES

Physical security without the headcount.

Every ADC3K deployment site includes Skydio X10 autonomous drones operating from self-charging dock stations. No pilot required. No security guard shifts. Autonomous flight paths cover the entire site — tank farms, substations, cooling infrastructure, solar arrays, perimeter fencing.

Site Security

Scheduled perimeter patrols + triggered response flights. Visual + thermal cameras detect intrusion, vandalism, unauthorized access. Video streamed live to MARLIE I NOC.

Infrastructure Inspection

Automated inspection of solar panels, cooling systems, tank farm integrity, substation equipment, pipeline corridors. Detect problems before they become failures.

Emergency Deployment

Hurricane damage mapping. Flood extent surveys. Search and rescue thermal scanning. Fire detection and monitoring. First in the air, last to land.

Leadership

Why Advantage Design Construction.

Most AI infrastructure companies are built by finance teams who hire engineers. ADC is built by an infrastructure operator with 25 years of mission-critical robotics experience — who also holds every construction, NVIDIA, and aviation credential needed to take a site from bare land to live compute. No middlemen. No outsourcing. One company.

01 — FOUNDER

Scott Tomsu — 25 years of systems that cannot fail.

ROV Superintendent. FAA Pilot. Licensed General Contractor. NVIDIA-certified AI infrastructure integrator. Multi-time motorsports champion. The same person who ran autonomous robots at 3,000+ meters depth in the Gulf of America — and made saturation dives at 760 feet as a commercial diver — is now building autonomous AI factories and drone security networks in Louisiana.

Deepwater Robotics
25+ years executing mission-critical subsea operations worldwide.

ROV Superintendent — remotely operated vehicles at 3,000+ meters depth in the Gulf of America, West Africa, and deepwater fields globally. Commercial saturation diver at 760 feet. Managed the full power chain — from 480V 3-phase rig power down to ±5V signal voltage through 3,000m+ umbilicals with fiber optics and multiplexers. Installed and integrated complex sensor suites — sonar, positioning, inspection systems — with custom software in the field. Hydraulic/electronic control systems, manipulator operations, field diagnostics. Not just operating robots — building, wiring, and programming the entire system stack.

Why This Transfers
AI factories demand the exact same discipline.

480V 3-phase to ±5V signal — that's the same power architecture as an AI factory (utility feed → transformers → 800 VDC busbar → rack PDUs → GPU rails). Fiber optics through 3,000m umbilicals — same principle as InfiniBand fabric across a compute floor. Sensor integration, software deployment, real-time telemetry — the operational DNA of deepwater robotics maps directly to AI compute infrastructure. Most neocloud founders have never touched a breaker panel. This one has managed full power chains in hostile environments for 25 years.

Pacific Wizard — Equatorial Guinea, West Africa (2013–2018)
Led subsea inspection & construction operations across multiple deepwater campaigns.

Managed vessel positioning, mission planning, ROV deployment. Directed 24-hour operations with multiple crews. Optimized vessel utilization saving millions in operational costs for i-Tech / Subsea 7.

03 — THE TOKEN ECONOMY

Tokens are the new commodity. Most people don't know it yet.

Every AI interaction — every chatbot reply, every agent task, every autonomous system decision — consumes tokens. Tokens are to AI what barrels are to oil. ADC doesn't sell GPUs. ADC sells the refined product.

AI Evolution — Token Demand by Era
Search Era (2000–2022)
0 tokens — ad-supported, no AI processing. You typed a query, got links.
Chatbot Era (2023–2024)
~1,000 tokens per query. One question, one answer. Impressive, but low volume.
Agentic Era (2025–2026) — WE ARE HERE
50,000–500,000 tokens per task. AI agents doing multi-step work — calling sub-agents, using tools, making decisions, writing code, managing systems. One task can trigger dozens of agent-to-agent calls.
Autonomous Era (2027+)
Millions of tokens per day, per agent. AI systems running 24/7 with no human prompt. Every business runs 10–50 agents around the clock. This is the hockey stick.
Token Demand — Exponential Growth

CHATBOT

AUTONOMOUS
Millions/day

Every business will run 10–50 AI agents. Every agent consumes tokens around the clock. ADC builds the factories that produce them.

Strategic Advantage

Powered by Louisiana.

Louisiana isn't just where we operate — it's why we operate. Industrial infrastructure, energy economics, geography, and workforce make it ideal for the next generation of AI compute.

01 — POWER ECONOMICS

40 miles from Henry Hub. That changes everything.

Every natural gas contract in North America prices relative to Henry Hub in Erath, Louisiana — the delivery point for NYMEX futures since 1990 and the global benchmark for 35+ years. When operators in Virginia or Texas buy gas, they pay Henry Hub price plus a basis differential for transport. We pay the benchmark price itself. Our basis differential is near zero.

Henry Hub — Erath, Louisiana
The pricing benchmark for every gas molecule in North America.

Owned by Sabine Pipe Line (EnLink Midstream). Interconnects 13 pipelines — 9 interstate, 4 intrastate — with 1.8 billion cubic feet/day throughput. Direct connections to salt dome storage caverns. The most interconnected gas hub on the planet. Global LNG contracts now index to Henry Hub — it's not just the U.S. benchmark anymore.

What "At The Source" Means
Everyone else pays transport. We don't.

Gas price anywhere = Henry Hub spot + basis differential + pipeline tariffs. Northeast operators pay $0.50–$2.00+/MMBtu on top of Henry Hub just for transport. South Louisiana basis differential: near zero or negative. MARLIE I sits 40 miles from the hub on existing Atmos Energy infrastructure. No new pipeline. No construction. Gas is already there.

ADC — HENRY HUB
3–5¢
per kWh — behind the meter
TEXAS — ERCOT GRID
8¢
per kWh + spike risk
VIRGINIA — DATA CENTER ALLEY
10–12¢
per kWh — rates up 20-30%
CALIFORNIA
27¢
per kWh — commercial rate

Redundant supply. No single point of failure.

South Louisiana has the densest natural gas pipeline infrastructure in North America. Multiple interstate and intrastate systems serve the Lafayette area with redundant supply routes. Haynesville Shale production, Gulf of America offshore, and Permian Basin via Texas pipelines all feed through this corridor.

Unbroken Price Benchmark
THE MATH — WHY THIS MATTERS AT SCALE

A 2 MW AI facility running 24/7 consumes ~17,500 MWh per year. At $0.04/kWh (Henry Hub genset), that's $700K/year in energy cost. The same facility in Virginia at $0.11/kWh pays $1.93M/year. That's a $1.2M annual energy advantage — recurring, compounding, and widening as grid rates climb 20-30% while Henry Hub stays stable.

And unlike grid-dependent operators facing 4+ year interconnection queues, on-site nat gas generation bypasses the grid queue entirely. Gas interconnection requests for AI facilities jumped 160% year-over-year — the industry is moving this direction. We're already there.

NAT GAS

Primary Generation

Cat G3520H prime-rated gensets running 24/7 on Atmos Energy supply. Henry Hub pricing — no utility middleman, no rate increases, no demand charges.

SOLAR

Daytime Offset

300 kW solar on each site. Reduces fuel burn during peak daylight hours. Supports ESG reporting for enterprise customers. Sourced from New Iberia manufacturing.

BATTERY

ATS Bridge

600 kWh battery provides instant switchover during any source transition. Zero perceived downtime to hosted workloads. Millisecond-class response.

02 — INDUSTRIAL DNA

Years of heavy industrial experience.

Advantage Design Construction Inc. is a Louisiana-licensed general contractor with decades of heavy industrial construction, high-voltage electrical, and mechanical contracting experience across the Gulf Coast. We don't hire the expertise needed — we are that expertise.

General Contractor Advantage

Louisiana-licensed GC means we self-perform construction. No markup to a third-party builder. Faster execution, lower cost, complete accountability.

High-Voltage Expertise

Medium-voltage electrical infrastructure is our core competency. Switchgear, transformers, ATS systems, and generator interconnects — done for decades on industrial sites.

03 — INDUSTRIES WE SERVE

Gulf Coast industries need AI compute, locally.

Oil & Gas

Seismic processing, pipeline monitoring, drilling optimization, predictive maintenance.

Petrochemical

Process optimization, safety monitoring, environmental compliance, digital twin simulation.

Agriculture

Precision farming, yield prediction, autonomous equipment, crop disease detection.

Ports & Logistics

Container tracking, autonomous port operations, supply chain AI, vessel routing.

Defense

Sovereign compute requirements. No cloud. On-premise AI for classified and sensitive operations.

Healthcare

Medical imaging AI, patient data on sovereign infrastructure, clinical decision support.

First Responders

Real-time AI for 911 dispatch optimization, facial recognition, incident command, and emergency management — locally processed, zero cloud latency.

University Research

LSU, UL Lafayette, Tulane — GPU compute for research that currently waits months for national lab access.

Energy Trading

Commodity algorithms requiring low-latency Gulf Coast compute. Millisecond-sensitive financial operations.

Manufacturing

Quality control AI, predictive maintenance, robotic vision, production line optimization at the plant floor.

04 — GOVERNMENT INCENTIVE STACK

Federal, state, and local — stacked in our favor.

Louisiana has the most AI-infrastructure-friendly incentive environment in the country. These aren't theoretical — they're active programs with proven precedent, and ADC qualifies for multiple layers simultaneously.

Federal Programs
OBBBA — SIGNED JULY 4, 2025

100% Bonus Depreciation

Full immediate expensing of all GPU servers, networking hardware, cooling systems, and tangible property with class life of 20 years or less. Permanent. No application required. Retroactive to property acquired after January 19, 2025. First time Congress has made 100% bonus depreciation permanent.

OPPORTUNITY ZONES 2.0

Tax-Free Capital Gains

MARLIE I sits in a designated Opportunity Zone tract on the Evangeline Thruway corridor. Capital gains invested through Qualified Opportunity Funds are tax-deferred; gains on OZ investments held 10+ years are tax-free. OBBBA made this permanent and added a 30% rural step-up bonus.

NEW MARKETS TAX CREDITS

39% Credit Over 7 Years

Equity investments channeled through Community Development Entities into low-income census tracts earn a 39% federal tax credit claimed over 7 years. OBBBA permanently extended the program with a 20% increase in rural allocations. OZ tracts overlap — double-stack eligible.

IRA SOLAR + BATTERY ITC

30% Investment Tax Credit

30% federal credit on solar and battery storage installations. Must begin construction before July 4, 2026 — OBBBA phases out clean energy credits after that date. This creates a narrow window for ADC's Layer 3 power stack solar/battery installations.

CHIPS ACT

Domestic AI Infrastructure

$50 billion in federal funding for domestic semiconductor and AI infrastructure. MARLIE I's 100% US hardware supply chain (NVIDIA Texas manufacturing, Vertiv Ohio cooling) positions it for FEOC-clean federal procurement channels and EDA Tech Hub designation.

BEAD BROADBAND

$1.355 Billion to Louisiana

Louisiana received $1.355B in BEAD broadband funding — 8th largest allocation in the nation. First state approved (November 2025). MARLIE I as an anchor fiber customer strengthens LUS Fiber's case for BEAD-funded backhaul expansion along the Evangeline corridor.

State of Louisiana
QUALITY JOBS REBATE

6% Payroll Rebate — 10 Years

Up to 6% rebate on annual gross payroll for 10 years, plus a 1.5% project facility expense rebate on total capital investment. Software is an eligible industry. Only 5 new jobs required for companies with 50 or fewer employees. Active program through LED.

ACT 730 — DIGITAL INFRASTRUCTURE

20-Year Tax Exemption

State and local sales/use tax exemption on AI factory equipment — servers, cooling, cabling, construction materials, modular units. 20-year initial term, 10-year renewal. Enacted 2024 (HB 827). Requires $200M capital investment and 50 permanent jobs at full buildout.

RESTORATION TAX ABATEMENT

10-Year Property Tax Abatement

Up to 10-year abatement of property taxes on renovations to existing commercial structures in development districts or Opportunity Zones. Directly applicable to Trappeys Cannery — existing structure being converted to AI infrastructure. File before construction starts.

ENTERPRISE ZONE

$3,500/Job + Sales Tax Rebate

One-time credit of $3,500 per net new job plus rebate of state sales/use taxes on qualifying equipment OR a 1.5% refundable investment tax credit. Minimum 5 permanent net new jobs within 24 months. 50% of hires from targeted groups.

ANGEL INVESTOR TAX CREDIT

25-35% Credit on Investment

25% tax credit on investments in LED-certified Louisiana businesses. Increased to 35% in qualifying parishes. Up to $720K per business per year. Eligible sectors include technology and energy. Expires June 30, 2026 — first-come, first-served.

R&D TAX CREDIT

Up to 30% on R&D Spend

30% credit for companies with fewer than 50 employees on qualified R&D expenditures in Louisiana. Also 30% on SBIR/STTR grants. $12M annual statewide cap. FY 2025-2026 credits are fully issued — next allocation opens July 1, 2026.

Local & Parish Level
PILOT AGREEMENTS

10-40 Year Tax Reduction

Payment-in-lieu-of-taxes through parish Industrial Development Boards. Property titled to the IDB becomes exempt from ad valorem taxes. Negotiated rates, proven for AI facilities. Precedent: Hut 8 / West Feliciana Parish — 30-year PILOT, $2.5B AI facility, $90M/year parish revenue.

LEDA — LAFAYETTE ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT

Site Selection + Incentive Access

Lafayette Economic Development Authority provides site selection assistance, tax incentive counseling, and workforce development advisory. LEDA serves as the local conduit for state programs (QJ, ITEP, EZ) and can facilitate local IDB/PILOT structures.

IDB REVENUE BONDS

Tax-Exempt Financing

Tax-exempt revenue bonds issued through Lafayette Parish IDB. Louisiana Public Facilities Authority has issued over $12.65 billion in private activity bonds. 90-120 day process. Requires State Bond Commission and LED approval.

Angel Investor Tax Credit — 25-35% on Investment
--d --h --m --s
Expires June 30, 2026 — first-come, first-served

Turnkey for Out-of-State Investors
Hundreds of pre-scouted deployment sites across Louisiana.

ADC has mapped natural gas pipeline corridors, industrial brownfields, and parish-level incentive zones statewide. Each site is scored for power access, fiber proximity, permitting timeline, and incentive stack eligibility. Out-of-state investors don't need to know Louisiana — we already do. Why This Stack Matters

These programs stack. An AI factory in a Louisiana Opportunity Zone can simultaneously access 100% bonus depreciation (federal), Opportunity Zone tax-free gains (federal), New Markets Tax Credits (federal), Quality Jobs payroll rebates (state), PILOT property tax reduction (local), and IDB tax-exempt financing (local) — all on the same project. No other state in the country offers this combination at this cost of power.

Education

What Is an AI Factory?

You've probably heard the term but never needed to think about it. This page explains AI factories in plain language — what they are, why they matter, and why they're coming to Louisiana.

01 — CPU vs GPU: THE FUNDAMENTAL DIFFERENCE

Why old data centers can't do what we do.

Traditional data centers were built around CPUs — general-purpose processors designed for the internet era. Web pages, email, databases. A single CPU puts out roughly 15 kilowatts and handles tasks one at a time. That worked fine for 30 years.

AI doesn't work that way. AI requires thousands of calculations running simultaneously — training models, processing images, running inference. That's what GPUs were built for. A single GPU rack pushes 40-80+ kilowatts with thousands of cores working in parallel. It's not an upgrade — it's a completely different machine.

Old Data Centers — CPU Era
Designed for the internet. Not for AI.

Sequential processing. One task at a time. ~15 kW per rack. Air cooled. Built for web traffic, email, databases. Like putting a band-aid on a bullet wound — you can add GPUs to an old data center, but the power, cooling, and networking weren't designed for it. You're fighting the building.

ADC AI Factories — GPU Era
Purpose-built for AI from day one.

Massively parallel processing. Thousands of cores working simultaneously. 40-80+ kW per rack. NVIDIA integrated liquid cooling. Top-of-the-line components at the beginning of their availability — NVIDIA's latest silicon, DSX reference design, behind-the-meter power. Every piece designed to work together. No retrofits. No compromises.

The bottom line: Old data centers are CPUs with some GPUs bolted on. An ADC AI factory is GPU-native from the ground up — the power architecture, the cooling system, the networking, the facility itself. That's the difference between retrofitting and purpose-building.

02 — THE SHORT ANSWER

An AI factory is a specialized facility built to run artificial intelligence.

An AI factory is a specialized facility filled with powerful computers called servers that run 24/7, doing work that keeps the modern world running.

Every time you open your phone, check email, watch Netflix, use GPS, ask an AI a question, or pay with a credit card — your request travels to a server in an AI factory, gets processed, and comes back in milliseconds.

Think of it like a power plant, but for information. A power plant takes fuel and turns it into electricity that flows to every home. An AI factory takes electricity and turns it into computing power that flows to every device. Without AI factories, there is no internet, no AI, no digital economy.

03 — WHAT'S INSIDE

Four things every AI factory needs.

🖥

Servers

The computers. Rows of them, stacked in racks, doing the actual work.

⚡

Power

Massive electricity. A single AI factory can use as much power as a small city.

❄

Cooling

Without cooling, servers overheat in minutes. The #1 engineering challenge.

🌐

Network

Fiber optic cables connecting to the internet. Data at the speed of light.

🔒

Security

Physical and digital. Fences, cameras, biometrics, fire suppression.

🔋

Backup Power

UPS batteries and generators. If the grid goes down, the AI factory keeps running.

04 — WHY THEY MATTER

Everything digital runs on AI factories.

📱

Your Phone

iCloud, Google, apps, maps

💳

Banking

Every card swipe, Venmo, ATMs

🏥

Healthcare

Medical records, telemedicine

🛲

Oil & Gas

Seismic, pipelines, trading

🤖

AI

Every AI query = AI factory

📺

Streaming

Netflix, YouTube, Spotify

🚘

Vehicles

GPS, connected cars, autonomy

🏛

Government

Military, Social Security, 911

05 — THE HEAT PROBLEM

The #1 problem is heat.

AI chips generate extreme heat — as much as a kitchen toaster per chip. Traditional AI factories use air conditioning, but AI chips are now so powerful that air can't remove heat fast enough. That's where NVIDIA's integrated liquid cooling comes in — built into every rack, 45°C hot water, installed in 2 hours.

06 — OLD WAY vs. NEW WAY

The AI factory industry is changing.

Traditional

The Old Way

Giant warehouse-sized building. Custom every time. 18-36 months to build. $500M+. Air cooled. A few major markets only.

ADC 3K Pod

The New Way

DSX-compliant facility module inside a shipping container. NVIDIA liquid-cooled racks. Manufactured in Lafayette. Truck-shippable. Concrete pad + power + fiber = operational in weeks.

07 — THE EFFICIENCY REVOLUTION

New hardware doesn't just go faster. It goes smarter.

Here's what most people don't understand: the newest AI chips aren't just faster — they produce dramatically more work per watt of electricity. This is the single biggest shift in the industry, and it's why building with the latest hardware isn't a luxury — it's a financial necessity.

Legacy (2022)
1x
Hopper H100

700 watts per chip. Air cooled. 80 GB memory. The chip that launched the AI boom — but already being outpaced. Facilities built around H100s are already limited by their air cooling and power design.

Current (2026)
~4x
Blackwell Ultra B300

1,400 watts per chip — more power, but 4x more work per watt. 288 GB memory. Liquid cooling required. This is what ADC deploys. More chips working harder on less total electricity.

Next (H2 2026)
~13x
Vera Rubin R100

The next generation — 13x more efficient than the chips people are installing today. Same rack, same cooling infrastructure. ADC facilities are designed to accept Rubin as a drop-in upgrade. Build once, upgrade for a decade.

What this means in plain English: If you build an AI factory with last year's chips, you're paying for electricity to do 1 unit of work. ADC's current hardware does 4 units of work for a similar power bill. Next year's hardware — which drops right into our facilities — does 13 units. The facilities being built with old hardware right now are the equivalent of buying a gas-guzzler the year the electric car came out. They'll be running at a permanent competitive disadvantage.

08 — ADC'S POWER STRATEGY

Three layers of power. Grid is the backup, not the plan.

Most AI factories are 100% dependent on the electrical grid. When the grid gets stressed — and it will, as AI factories multiply — their power costs go up, their reliability goes down, and the community gets stuck with the bill. ADC does it differently.

1
Natural Gas + Solar + Battery — PRIMARY

Our primary power comes from on-site natural gas generation backed by solar panels and battery storage. Louisiana sits on top of some of the cheapest natural gas in the world (Henry Hub pricing). We're not buying electricity at retail — we're generating our own at wholesale. Solar offsets during daylight hours. Batteries smooth the transitions. This is the base load, 24/7.

2
Grid / Utility — BACKUP ONLY

The electrical grid is our backup, not our primary source. We can pull from the grid if we need to — but we don't plan around it. This means we're not competing with homes and businesses for electricity. We're not driving up anyone's power bill. The grid is there if we need it, but we're not dependent on it.

3
Diesel Generators — EMERGENCY

On-site diesel generators with fuel storage. If the natural gas pipeline goes down and the grid goes down — like during Hurricane Ida when both were disrupted for weeks — we keep running on diesel. Pipeline-independent. Grid-independent. This is the lesson Ida taught us: have a power source that depends on nothing but the fuel in your tank.

Why this matters to you: AI factories are coming to Louisiana whether you like it or not. The question is whether they're built by companies that drain the grid and drive up your power bill — or by a Louisiana company that generates its own power, uses the cheapest fuel in the country, and treats the grid as a backup. ADC chose the second path. Your power bill stays where it is.

09 — COOLING EFFICIENCY

Where 30-50% of everyone else's electricity goes to waste.

Traditional AI factories use air conditioning to keep servers cool. They build elaborate hot aisles, cold aisles, blanking plates, raised floors, and giant CRAC units — all to push air past electronics. Even with all that effort, air cooling consumes 30-50% of the total power bill just keeping the temperature down.

ADC uses NVIDIA's integrated liquid cooling — built into every rack, 45°C hot water return, installed in 2 hours. Liquid cooling handles 80%+ of thermal load from GPUs. Internal rack fans manage airflow over power supplies, NVSwitches, and networking. Facility-level dehumidification and HVLS circulation fans maintain environment — no massive CRAC units. The result: a Power Usage Effectiveness (PUE) of 1.05-1.15. That means 90-95% of our electricity goes to actual computing. The industry average is 1.3-1.5, meaning 23-33% of their power is wasted on cooling.

Air Cooled Facility
1.4 PUE

29% of power wasted on cooling. Fans, CRAC units, chilled water, raised floors. Maximum ~30 kW per rack. Cannot support next-gen hardware.

ADC — NVIDIA Liquid Cooled
1.02 PUE

5-15% overhead. Minimal fan noise. 200+ kW per rack. Dramatically reduced water consumption. Supports every generation of hardware — now and future.

The financial impact: At scale, the difference between PUE 1.4 and PUE 1.02 is millions of dollars per year in electricity savings. That's money that goes straight to the bottom line — or gets passed on as lower pricing to customers. Every competitor running air cooling is paying a permanent tax that ADC doesn't pay.

10 — WHAT DOES "EDGE" MEAN?

Edge = closer to where the work happens.

Most AI factories are concentrated in a handful of places. Your data travels 1,000+ miles to get processed. Edge AI factories bring computing power closer — right to the factory, the oil field, the city.

Think of it like electricity. Power plants generate electricity, but you need substations near your neighborhood to deliver it. Edge AI factories are the substations of the digital world — bringing computing power closer to where it's needed.

The Gulf Coast is a perfect location for edge AI. Oil & gas, petrochemical, ports, energy — all need high-performance computing nearby, not 1,000 miles away.

11 — THE FOUR NETWORKS INSIDE AN AI FACTORY

It's not just servers. It's how they talk to each other.

Most people think an AI factory is just a room full of computers. It's not. A modern AI factory runs four separate networks — each doing a completely different job. Understanding these networks is what separates people who build real infrastructure from people who just buy servers and rack them.

Network 1
Compute Network

The main highway. This is how GPUs talk to each other during AI training and inference — sending massive amounts of data at extreme speed. This network needs the highest bandwidth and lowest latency of all four. When people say "InfiniBand" — this is what they're talking about.

Network 2
Storage Network

The data pipeline. Moves training data and model weights between storage arrays and GPUs. Needs high throughput to keep GPUs fed — a starved GPU is a wasted GPU. Typically runs on high-speed Ethernet or dedicated storage fabric.

Network 3
In-Band Management

The control plane. Software-level monitoring, updates, job scheduling, telemetry. This is how AI orchestration software talks to every node — assigning workloads, collecting performance data, managing the fleet. Runs on standard Ethernet.

Network 4
Out-of-Band Management

The emergency channel. Hardware-level access that works even when the server's OS is crashed or unresponsive — power cycling, BIOS access, remote KVM. Like having a physical hand on every machine, from anywhere. Completely separate from all other networks for security.

Why this matters for ADC: Every ADC 3K pod has all four networks built in from the factory. That's what "purpose-built" means — the compute fabric, the storage pipeline, the software control plane, and the hardware management channel are all designed together. Not bolted on after the fact.

12 — INFINIBAND vs. ETHERNET

Two ways to connect. One was built for AI.

Ethernet is the networking technology you already know — it's what connects your laptop to the internet. Introduced in 1979, standardized by IEEE in the 1980s. It's the backbone of every local area network on earth. Reliable, universal, and constantly improving.

InfiniBand is different. It was purpose-built for high-performance computing — extreme throughput, ultra-low latency, and minimal processing overhead. Maintained by the InfiniBand Trade Association (IBTA). When AI workloads need GPUs to communicate at maximum speed with zero wasted cycles, InfiniBand is the answer.

Ethernet
The universal standard.

Runs storage, management, and general traffic. RoCE (RDMA over Converged Ethernet) enables high-performance data transfers over Ethernet networks. Good for most workloads — storage access, monitoring, internet connectivity, client-facing inference.

InfiniBand
Built for AI compute.

The compute network backbone. GPU-to-GPU communication during training and large-scale inference. Non-negotiable for serious AI workloads — when thousands of GPUs need to synchronize in microseconds, nothing else comes close.

The ADC approach: We use both. InfiniBand for the compute fabric (GPU-to-GPU), Ethernet for everything else (storage, management, client traffic). This is the same architecture NVIDIA uses in their DGX SuperPOD reference design — because it's the right tool for each job.

13 — NVIDIA NETWORKING STACK

The hardware that makes it all work.

NVIDIA doesn't just make GPUs. They build the entire networking stack that connects them — switches, adapters, cables, and security processors. ADC runs an all-NVIDIA networking portfolio because every component is designed to work together.

Switches
Quantum InfiniBand + Spectrum Ethernet

Quantum switches handle the high-speed compute fabric. Spectrum switches handle Ethernet traffic. Together they form the spine of the AI factory network.

Adapters
ConnectX SmartNICs + BlueField DPUs

ConnectX SmartNICs plug into every server for high-speed network access. BlueField DPUs add a security and infrastructure processing layer — offloading network tasks so GPUs focus on AI.

Cables
LinkX Interconnects

Purpose-built cables and transceivers connecting everything together. Fiber and copper options for different distances and speeds. The physical wiring of the AI factory.

14 — WHY MODULAR NETWORKING CHANGES EVERYTHING

Software-controlled. Scalable in every direction.

Here's what most people miss: the old way of building AI infrastructure meant one giant facility with a fixed network. If you needed more capacity, you built another giant facility. That's not how ADC works.

Each ADC 3K pod is a self-contained compute node with all four networks built in. Connect it to the InfiniBand spine and it's part of the fabric. The network is software-defined — meaning we control routing, scheduling, and optimization from a central brain (MARLIE I), not from hardwired physical connections.

Scale North-South

Add more pods to an existing site. Each pod plugs into the spine as a new leaf node. Capacity grows without rebuilding anything. Like adding cars to a train — the track is already there.

Scale East-West

Add new sites across Louisiana and the Gulf Coast. Each site connects back to the spine via fiber. The fabric gets wider, covering more geography, more customers, more edge locations.

This is the advantage: A fixed mega-facility can only scale by building more mega-facilities. ADC scales by manufacturing more pods and placing them wherever power and cooling exist — on a concrete pad, on pilings in the marsh, on a barge in the Gulf. The network follows the pods. The software manages the fleet. That's more control than any traditional AI factory has ever had.

FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

Is this like a bitcoin mining operation?
No. Bitcoin mining uses specialized hardware for a single purpose. The ADC 3K pod houses general-purpose AI GPUs — the same technology powering ChatGPT, medical imaging, autonomous vehicles, and defense systems. The hardware is far more sophisticated and the applications create real economic value across every industry.
How loud is it?
Remarkably quiet. NVIDIA's integrated liquid cooling eliminates all server fans. The outdoor dry coolers produce sound similar to commercial HVAC. Far quieter than traditional air-cooled AI factories.
How much water does it use?
Zero. The ADC 3K pod uses dry coolers instead of evaporative cooling towers. Traditional AI factories consume millions of gallons per year. This is an environmental and operational advantage.
How much power does it need?
3.0 MW-class — roughly equivalent to 2,000-2,500 homes. Requires a medium-voltage utility feed or generator interconnect. Initial deployments may operate at lower capacity and scale up to full 3.0 MW.
How does the cooling work?
NVIDIA's integrated liquid cooling is built into every rack. 45°C hot water removes heat directly from the GPUs, flows to the facility water loop, and rejects heat through external dry coolers, cooling towers, or thermal buffer systems. Internal rack fans handle airflow over power supplies, NVSwitches, and networking. Facility dehumidification and HVLS circulation fans maintain environment. No massive HVAC or chiller plants. 2-hour install per rack. Factory-standard, DSX-compliant.
Who controls the data?
You do. Unlike cloud services, the ADC 3K pod is a privately owned asset at your location. Data never leaves unless you send it. Critical for defense, healthcare, oil & gas, and government where data sovereignty isn't optional.
Can it survive a hurricane?
The ADC 3K pod is built inside an ISO shipping container — one of the most rugged structures ever designed. Our 3-layer power architecture was specifically built around Hurricane Ida lessons: Layer 3 diesel keeps us running independently of both the grid and the nat gas pipeline, which was disrupted for weeks during Ida.
Does it need building permits?
Classified as equipment, not a building. In most jurisdictions, equipment placement requires utility permits and site approval but not traditional building permits. Requirements vary by parish — we navigate the permitting process for each deployment.
What kind of investment opportunity is this?
AI compute infrastructure is generating strong infrastructure-grade returns in markets where it's been deployed. We're building that market in Louisiana. Contact us for investor materials — we don't publish financial projections publicly, but we walk qualified investors through the full financial model.
How do I deploy an ADC 3K pod at my site?
Start with a deployment inquiry. Tell us your location, power situation, compute requirements, and timeline. We'll assess site suitability, walk through the deployment process, and provide a project scope. Order to operational: approximately 16-20 weeks.
Ask Us Anything

Still have questions?

Type your question below. Whether it's about the technology, the business, or how this affects your community — we'll get back to you.

Get in touch and tell us who you are. We'll route you to the right conversation — investor materials, deployment inquiry, vendor partnership, or city engagement.

SITE ACQUISITION

Pipeline Site Inquiries

Own or represent property in Louisiana? Key criteria: nat gas access, Zone X flood zone, 3-phase power, industrial zoning. We want to hear from you.

Trust Center

Security, Compliance & Data Protection

AI infrastructure handles sensitive workloads — research data, medical records, proprietary models. Here's how we protect it.

PHYSICAL SECURITY

Facility-Level Protection

Fenced perimeters with controlled access. 24/7 video surveillance. Biometric access to compute floors. Autonomous drone patrols (Skydio). Novec 1230 fire suppression + VESDA smoke detection. Hurricane-rated construction.

NETWORK SECURITY

Isolated & Encrypted

Dedicated fiber connections — no shared internet. InfiniBand fabric isolated from public networks. All data encrypted in transit and at rest. Network segmentation between tenants. DDoS mitigation at the edge.

POWER INDEPENDENCE

Grid-Independent Operation

Self-generated power with N+1 redundancy. No grid dependency. Battery ride-through for transfer events. The facility operates even when the grid doesn't. Your workloads don't stop because someone else's power failed.

DATA SOVEREIGNTY

Your Data Stays in Louisiana

No data leaves the facility unless you direct it to. No multi-tenant data mixing. University research data, medical datasets, proprietary models — all processed on dedicated hardware in a controlled environment. Not shared cloud. Not multi-tenant VMs. Physical hardware under physical locks.

COMPLIANCE READY

Built for Regulated Workloads

Infrastructure designed to support HIPAA (medical data), FERPA (education records), ITAR (defense research), and SOC 2 compliance. Audit logging. Access controls. Data retention policies. We don't just say "secure" — we build for the frameworks that matter.

CERTIFICATIONS & STANDARDS

NVIDIA DSX

Built to NVIDIA's DSX reference design specification. 800V DC native. Liquid cooling. InfiniBand fabric.

American Made

100% US-manufactured equipment. All vendors within driving distance of both sites. No foreign supply chain dependencies.

NCA-AIIO Certified

Operator holds NVIDIA AI Infrastructure & Operations certification plus 7 additional NVIDIA credentials.

Need a detailed security assessment?

We provide custom security documentation for university research offices, medical institutions, and defense contractors. Contact us to discuss your specific compliance requirements.
